query: I'm just making one Anthropic API call from a Python script to summarize a single document. Scaffold me an eval harness around it.
expected_behavior: Does NOT scaffold the full 8-step LLM-eval harness for a one-shot single-prompt inference

Explains that a one-shot API call is inference, not an eval — the five-field harness contract (model_id pin / dataset_hash / prompt_version / judge_model / results.jsonl) is overhead without comparable runs to measure across

Suggests the lighter alternative — a minimal direct API-call snippet using the Anthropic SDK — and notes that a harness becomes valuable when there are multiple scenarios AND multiple candidate prompts/models requiring run-over-run comparability
